_comment: Reference document — not consumed directly by Terraform. The active rate rule lives in modules/waf/main.tf. Sync changes manually.
name: RateLimitPerIp
rationale: An unauthenticated client should never need more than ~6.7 req/s sustained. Real users with bursty SPA navigation peak at ~30 req in a few seconds, well under 2000/5min. The number gives generous headroom for legitimate operators on the operations console while shutting down obvious scrapers and credential-stuffing bots.
tuningGuidance: Watch the `<name_prefix>-RateLimit` CloudWatch metric. Sustained > 0 = active blocking; investigate the source IPs.
If a legit office NAT is being rate-limited, prefer adding an IP set allowlist rule with a higher priority instead of raising the global limit.
Authenticated API rate limits (per-org / per-user) live in the application layer — Clerk + custom middleware — NOT here. WAF is the perimeter; the app enforces tenancy-aware quotas.
